examples.title: Two-Phase Commit Protocol
examples.description: Pseudocode showing coordinator and participant roles in 2PC
examples.code:
// COORDINATOR
procedure twoPhaseCommit(transaction):
 // Phase 1: Prepare
 send PREPARE to all participants
 votes = collectVotes()
 
 if all votes = YES then:
 // Phase 2: Commit
 send COMMIT to all participants
 collectAcknowledgments()
 return SUCCESS
 else:
 send ABORT to all participants
 collectAcknowledgments()
 return ABORTED

// PARTICIPANT
procedure onPrepare(transaction):
 if canCommit(transaction) then:
 lockResources()
 writeToLog(READY)
 send VOTE_YES to coordinator
 waitForDecision()
 else:
 send VOTE_NO to coordinator
 return ABORTED

procedure onCommit():
 makeChangesPermanent()
 releaseLocks()
 send ACK to coordinator

procedure onAbort():
 rollbackChanges()
 releaseLocks()
 send ACK to coordinator
examples.explanation: Coordinator orchestrates the two phases: Prepare asks all participants if they can commit, and only if all vote Yes does it send Commit. Otherwise sends Abort. Participants lock resources and wait for coordinator's decision.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) messages where n = number of participants

examples.title: Three-Phase Commit Protocol
examples.code:
// COORDINATOR
procedure threePhaseCommit(transaction):
 // Phase 1: CanCommit
 send CAN_COMMIT to all participants
 votes = collectVotes()
 
 if all votes = YES then:
 // Phase 2: PreCommit
 send PRE_COMMIT to all participants
 collectAcknowledgments()
 
 // Phase 3: DoCommit
 send DO_COMMIT to all participants
 collectAcknowledgments()
 return SUCCESS
 else:
 send ABORT to all participants
 return ABORTED

// PARTICIPANT
procedure onCanCommit():
 if canCommit() then:
 send VOTE_YES
 waitForPreCommit()
 else:
 send VOTE_NO

procedure onPreCommit():
 writeToLog(PREPARED_TO_COMMIT)
 send ACK
 waitForDoCommit()

procedure onDoCommit():
 commit()
 send ACK

procedure onAbort():
 abort()
 send ACK
examples.explanation: 3PC adds PreCommit phase between Prepare and Commit. If coordinator fails after PreCommit, participants know all were ready and can safely commit. This eliminates the blocking state.
examples.timeComplexity: O(3n) messages - three rounds of communication

examples.title: Saga Pattern Implementation
examples.description: Pseudocode for Saga pattern with compensating transactions
examples.explanation: Saga executes a sequence of local transactions. If any step fails, compensating transactions (reverse operations) are executed to undo completed work. Suitable for long-running distributed transactions.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for success, O(n²) worst case with compensation

examples.title: Nested Transaction Execution
examples.explanation: Nested transactions allow sub-transactions (children) to execute and commit independently. Parent coordinates children and may need to compensate if any child fails. Provides better modularity and partial commit capability.
examples.timeComplexity: O(d) where d = depth of nesting
